description:
A SINGLE UNINTERRUPTED PANORAMIC PAINTING of one continuous interior space inside THE SAME ANTIQUE-DEALER'S RESIDENCE as the cabinet/bureau scene of this game — same warm Art Déco palette, same museum-catalog rendering. The scene combines a STORAGE area on the LEFT THIRD and a CRAFTSMAN'S ATELIER on the CENTER + RIGHT THIRDS, sharing one single tall room.

ABSOLUTE FORBIDDEN ELEMENTS — DO NOT RENDER ANY OF THESE INSIDE THE IMAGE: no text, no labels, no captions, no percentages, no numbers, no measurement marks, no rulers, no ratios, no signatures, no watermarks, no annotation strips at the bottom or sides. The output is a pure painted scene with zero typography or numerical glyphs anywhere.

COMPOSITIONAL CONSTRAINT (FOR SPATIAL UNDERSTANDING ONLY — NOT TO BE RENDERED AS LABELS): this panorama will be viewed on a smartphone that swipes horizontally and snaps to THREE viewing thirds (LEFT THIRD, CENTER THIRD, RIGHT THIRD). For clean swipes, NO furniture, NO door, NO window, NO rack, NO shelving unit, NO hanging lamp, NO architectural feature may STRADDLE the two vertical boundaries between the thirds (the boundary between LEFT and CENTER, around one-third of the way from the left edge; the boundary between CENTER and RIGHT, around two-thirds of the way from the left). Each major object must sit ENTIRELY within ONE third. A narrow vertical strip of plain back wall + floor sits at each boundary (the breathing zones).

ARCHITECTURE — the viewer looks into the room straight-on at eye-level. The room has THREE visible walls forming a shallow box:
  • LEFT SIDE WALL: angles forward toward the viewer at the leftmost edge of the image (occupies the leftmost narrow vertical band, ~the first one-twelfth of the image width). The left wall recedes inward from the foreground-left toward the back-wall corner, so it visibly tilts in perspective — you see its face from a 3/4 angle. Plain warm cream plaster, exposed vertical wooden timber posts and corner bracing visible.
  • BACK WALL: flat, faces the viewer head-on, runs across the central portion of the image. Warm aged cream plaster with occasional exposed vertical wooden framing posts and timber studs (same wall treatment as the bureau cabinet of this game).
  • RIGHT SIDE WALL: angles forward toward the viewer at the rightmost edge of the image (occupies the rightmost narrow vertical band, ~the last one-twelfth of the image width). Mirrors the left side wall in perspective — recedes from the foreground-right back toward the back-wall corner, visible at a 3/4 angle. Same cream plaster with exposed vertical wooden timber posts and corner bracing.
  • CEILING: high (~4m), with one continuous exposed dark-wood beam running across the top of the image. Diagonal wooden corner braces visible where the side walls meet the ceiling.
  • FLOOR: ONE continuous warm wood plank floor (parquet vintage, slightly worn) runs unbroken across the bottom. The LEFT side of the floor (storage area) is CLEAN. The CENTER and RIGHT sides of the floor (atelier areas) show faint pale dust and small wood shavings.

LEFT THIRD — STORAGE: a tall INDUSTRIAL METAL STORAGE RACK standing AGAINST the back wall, parallel to the wall, front face square toward the viewer. Industrial vintage construction: dark forest-green painted steel angle-iron uprights (riveted), with five horizontal metal shelves stacked at evenly spaced heights from near the floor up to about head height. The rack is entirely contained within the LEFT THIRD with clear margin on both sides (well to the right of the left side wall, and clearly to the left of the boundary breathing zone). Most shelves are EMPTY (naked patinated metal, ready for composited items later). Light decoration: two plain brown cardboard storage boxes (closed) on the second-from-top shelf, and one small wooden crate on the topmost shelf. The other three shelves are entirely empty. No dust on this side.

CENTER THIRD — ATELIER WORKBENCH WITH STAINED-GLASS WINDOW: a substantial PROFESSIONAL ARTISAN WORKBENCH (établi de menuisier) centered in the middle third, fully contained within it with clear margin to both breathing zones. Real workshop workbench: GENEROUSLY WIDE (~1.8m visible width — a bit wider than a typical bench), ~85–90cm tall (top at hip-level for a standing adult), THICK worn-beech/oak top (8–10cm thick slab), four heavy square legs with sturdy lower stretchers, and a cast-iron BENCH VICE bolted to the front-left corner of the top with visible turning handle. The top is ENTIRELY EMPTY apart from the vice — bare worn wood. Above the workbench, a BEAUTIFUL WIDE WINDOW (slightly wider than the bench below, but still fully inside the center third): wooden frame in warm wood tones, ~1.6m wide, ~1.5m tall, sill resting just above the workbench top, divided into clean panes (3 vertical columns × 3 horizontal rows) by simple wooden mullions. Crowning the top of this window: a SPECTACULAR ART NOUVEAU STAINED-GLASS IMPOSTE (vitrail) about 30cm tall, depicting a STYLIZED BLACKSMITH'S ANVIL as the central motif, framed by curving organic Art Nouveau leaves and stylized tendrils. Warm jewel tones in the stained glass — amber, soft forest green, warm cream, hints of brass-gold — with visible lead came outlining each piece. Below the windowsill, a WIDE WOODEN TABLETTE (~30cm deep) protrudes generously into the room. On the tablette, tasteful decoration: a TERRACOTTA CLAY FLOWERPOT with warm-toned flowers (geraniums or marigolds, ochre/red petals) on the LEFT side; a TURNED-WOODEN CYLINDRICAL POT holding 4–5 paintbrushes with worn handles on the RIGHT side. Center of the tablette stays uncluttered. Through the clear panes, a SMALL CULTIVATED GARDEN is visible: rosebushes in bloom against a low stone garden wall, climbing roses on a wooden trellis, a paved garden path, terracotta pots — a cozy enclosed garden, NOT vast countryside. The floor below the workbench shows faint dust and small wood shavings.

RIGHT THIRD — ATELIER COMPOSITION (back-wall shelves + angled foreground workbench + ceiling lamps):
  • Against the BACK WALL (in the back portion of the right third, fully contained within the right third and clearly to the right of the center/right boundary breathing zone): a TALL OPEN WOODEN SHELF HUTCH made of warm wood, ~1.0m wide, ~2m tall, with FIVE horizontal wooden shelves between two vertical wooden side panels. The hutch sits on the floor and rises nearly to the ceiling beam. Tasteful decoration: a single TERRACOTTA JAR on the top shelf; TWO small plain wooden storage boxes on the middle shelf. The other three shelves are entirely empty.
  • In the FOREGROUND of the right third, a LONG ARTISAN WORKBENCH (longue table d'établi) viewed FROM A 3/4 ANGLE — its long axis runs DIAGONALLY from the back-wall area (around the boundary breathing zone, but staying clear of it) into the foreground-RIGHT-CORNER of the image. The workbench PROTRUDES forward into the viewer's space and CROPS OUT of frame at the bottom-right corner (only the back portion of its long axis is fully visible; the foreground end is partly cropped). Same construction as the central one — thick worn-oak top (~8–10cm thick), heavy square legs with sturdy lower stretchers, ~85–90cm tall, working height. Top is ENTIRELY EMPTY (no vice on this one — just bare wood with a few small holdfast holes). This long angled workbench is the FOREGROUND ANCHOR of the right third (analogous to the foreground writing desk in the bureau cabinet of this game).
  • HANGING FROM THE CEILING above the right third, ONE or TWO INDUSTRIAL VINTAGE METAL WORKSHOP PENDANT LAMPS (lustres d'atelier métalliques): black/dark cast-iron or steel enameled conical pendant shades (the classic Art Déco workshop lampshade — wide cone with a narrower neck, polished brass socket cap and chain), hanging on chains from the dark wood ceiling beam, suspended at about 1.8m above the floor (above the workbench top). They cast warm pools of light onto the angled workbench. If two lamps, they are spaced apart and both fully inside the right third.

LIGHTING: soft warm directional light from the upper-left, plus daylight + colored highlights through the center stained-glass window, plus the warm bulbs of the workshop pendant lamps on the right. The atelier half (center + right) is warmer and brighter; the storage side (left) is cleaner and slightly cooler. No people.

STYLE — IDENTICAL to the bureau cabinet panorama of this same game: vintage Art Déco illustration, museum-catalog feel, elegant ink line-art with subtle sepia + soft forest green color wash, cream parchment paper background with subtle paper grain texture, warm wood tones, brass accents. NOT industrial photography, NOT modern realism, NOT 3D render.

FINAL CONSTRAINTS:
(a) NO text, labels, captions, percentages, numbers, measurement marks, watermarks, or signatures anywhere in the image. The image is a pure painted scene.
(b) NO furniture, rack, window, shelf, or hanging lamp straddles either of the two third-boundaries (the one between left and center thirds, and the one between center and right thirds). Narrow vertical strips of plain back wall + floor sit at those boundaries.
(c) The LEFT side wall and the RIGHT side wall both angle forward in perspective at the extreme edges of the image (visible as 3/4-view receding walls), like in the bureau cabinet of this game.
(d) The RIGHT-THIRD workbench is a FOREGROUND ANCHOR at a 3/4 angle, with its long axis running diagonally toward the bottom-right corner of the image, partly cropping out of frame at the bottom-right.
(e) One or two industrial vintage METAL WORKSHOP PENDANT LAMPS hang on chains from the ceiling beam in the right third only.
(f) Workbenches are REAL workshop workbenches at standard working height (~85–90cm), thick worn-wood tops, sturdy heavy legs with stretchers. Central one has a bench vice; right (foreground) one has just holdfast holes.
(g) Center window has the Art Nouveau stained-glass anvil imposte above clear panes. Wide tablette below with terracotta flowerpot + flowers on the left, wooden brush pot on the right.
(h) Decorations are limited to: cardboard boxes + wooden crate on the storage rack (left); flowerpot + brush pot on the tablette (center); terracotta jar + two wooden boxes on the back-wall shelf hutch (right). All other shelves and workbench tops are empty.
(i) Dust + small wood shavings on the floor in the center and right portions; clean floor on the left.
(j) No transparent areas — PNG is fully opaque. No vertical seams, no panel borders, no black bars.
